snp3.4:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying among the Kosalans on the bank of the River Sundarika.
snp3.4:1.3:
And on that occasion,
the brahman Sundarika Bhāradvāja was offering a fire sacrifice and
performing a fire oblation on the bank of the River Sundarika.
snp3.4:1.4:
Then, having offered the fire sacrifice and performed the fire oblation,
he got up from his seat and looked around to the four directions,
snp3.4:1.5: (thinking,) “Who should eat the remains of the offering?”
snp3.4:1.6: He saw the Blessed One sitting not far away at the root of a tree with his head covered.
snp3.4:1.7:
On seeing him, he took the remains of the offering in his left hand and his water-pot in his right,
and went to the Blessed One.
snp3.4:2.1: Then the Blessed One, at the sound of the brahman Sundarika Bhāradvāja’s footsteps, uncovered his head.
snp3.4:2.2:
The brahman Sundarika Bhāradvāja (thinking,) “This venerable one is shaven.
This venerable one is a shaveling,” wanted to turn back.
snp3.4:2.3: But then the thought occurred to him,
snp3.4:2.4:
“Still, there are some brahmans who are shaven.
What if, having approached him, I were to ask his caste?”
snp3.4:2.5: So he went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, asked:
snp3.4:3.1: Then the Blessed One addressed the brahman Sundarika Bhāradvāja in verse:
snp3.4:4.1: “I’m not a brahman or king’s son,
snp3.4:4.2: not a merchant, or anyone at all.
snp3.4:4.3: Comprehending the clan of the run-of-the-mill,
snp3.4:4.4: having nothing, I wander by means of wisdom in the world.
snp3.4:5.1: Wearing my outer robe, I wander without home,
snp3.4:5.2: my hair shaven off, my mind entirely unbound,
snp3.4:5.4:
You ask me an inappropriate question about clan.”
Sundarika:
snp3.4:6.1: “But, sir, brahmans surely inquire of brahmans,
snp3.4:6.2:
‘Are you among the brahmans?’”
The Buddha:
snp3.4:7.3: I ask you the three lines of the Sāvitti
snp3.4:8.2: did seers, men, noble warriors, & brahmans
snp3.4:8.3:
—many of them here in the world— (first) arrange sacrifices to devas?”
The Buddha:
snp3.4:9.1: “Whoever has attained the end, an attainer-of-knowledge,
snp3.4:9.2:
should receive an oblation at the time of a sacrifice, his (sacrifice), I say, would succeed.”
Sundarika:
snp3.4:10.3: for we have seen an attainer-of-knowledge like you.
snp3.4:10.4: It’s from not seeing those like you
snp3.4:10.5:
that someone else eats the sacrificial cake.”
The Buddha:
snp3.4:11.1: “Therefore, brahman, as you are seeking your benefit,
snp3.4:11.3: Perhaps you may find here one at peace, with no anger, no desire, no affliction:
snp3.4:12.2: I desire to sacrifice, but I don’t understand
snp3.4:12.3:
where a sacrifice succeeds.
Teach me, sir.
snp3.4:13.1:
“In that case, brahman, lend ear.
I will teach you the Dhamma.
snp3.4:14.1:
Don’t inquire about birth.
Inquire about conduct.
snp3.4:14.2: As from wood, a fire is born,
snp3.4:14.3: so a sage, even from lowly birth —steadfast, restrained
snp3.4:14.4: through a sense of shame— becomes a thoroughbred.
snp3.4:15.1: One tamed by truth, endowed with self-control,
snp3.4:15.2: attained to the end of knowledge, having fulfilled the holy life:
snp3.4:15.3: To him, at the right time, you should bestow an offering,
snp3.4:15.4: to him a brahman aiming at merit should sacrifice.
snp3.4:16.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering,
snp3.4:16.4: to them a brahman aiming at merit should sacrifice.
snp3.4:17.1: Those devoid of passion, their faculties well-centered,
snp3.4:17.2: released like the moon from the grasp of an eclipse:
snp3.4:17.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering,
snp3.4:17.4: to them a brahman aiming at merit should sacrifice.
snp3.4:18.1: Unattached, they wander in the world,
snp3.4:18.3: To them, at the right time, you should bestow an offering,
snp3.4:18.4: to them a brahman aiming at merit should sacrifice.
snp3.4:19.2: who knows the end of birth & death,
snp3.4:19.3: totally unbound, cool as a pool of water:
snp3.4:19.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:20.1: Consonant among the consonant, far from the discordant,
snp3.4:20.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:21.1: In whom no deceptiveness dwells, no conceit,
snp3.4:21.2: devoid of greed, un- possessive, un- desiring,
snp3.4:21.3: his anger dispelled, his mind entirely unbound,
snp3.4:21.4: a brahman who has abandoned the stain of grief:
snp3.4:21.5: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:22.1: He has abandoned the homes of the mind,
snp3.4:22.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:23.1: Centered, he’s crossed over the flood,
snp3.4:23.2: he knows the Dhamma through the highest view,
snp3.4:23.3: effluents ended, bearing his last body:
snp3.4:23.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:24.1: Whose effluent of becoming and harsh speech
snp3.4:24.2: are destroyed, finished, do not exist—
snp3.4:24.3: he, an attainer-of-knowledge, everywhere totally released:
snp3.4:24.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:25.1: Gone beyond snares, for whom there are no snares,
snp3.4:25.2: who, among those attached to conceit, is unattached to conceit,
snp3.4:25.3: comprehending stress along with its field & its site:
snp3.4:25.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:26.2: gone beyond the views known by others,
snp3.4:26.3: who has no supports no mental objects at all:
snp3.4:26.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:27.1: In whom, having understood them, phenomena from high to low
snp3.4:27.2: are destroyed, finished, do not exist—
snp3.4:27.3: at peace, released in the ending of clinging:
snp3.4:27.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:28.1: Seeing the end & ending of fetters & birth,
snp3.4:28.2: having dispelled the path of passion without trace,
snp3.4:28.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:29.1: Who doesn’t contemplate self by means of self,
snp3.4:29.3: truly unperturbed, free from rigidity, free from doubt:
snp3.4:29.4: The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:30.1: Who has no conditions for delusion,
snp3.4:30.2: with knowledge & vision of all phenomena
snp3.4:30.4: having attained the unexcelled self-awakening, auspicious—
snp3.4:30.5: to that extent is the purity of a spirit:
snp3.4:30.6:
The Tathāgata deserves the sacrificial cake.”
Sundarika:
snp3.4:31.1: “And may my offering be a true offering
snp3.4:31.2: from having obtained an attainer-of-knowledge like you.
snp3.4:31.3: As Brahmā is my witness, may the Blessed One accept,
snp3.4:31.4:
may the Blessed One eat, my sacrificial cake.”
The Buddha:
snp3.4:32.1: “What’s been chanted over with verses shouldn’t be eaten by me.
snp3.4:32.2: That’s not the nature, brahman, of one who’s seen rightly.
snp3.4:32.3: What’s been chanted over with verses Awakened Ones reject.
snp3.4:32.4:
That being their Dhamma, brahman, this is their way of life.
Serve with other food & drink
snp3.4:33.2: his effluents ended, his anxiety stilled,
snp3.4:33.4:
for that is the field for one looking for merit.”
Sundarika:
snp3.4:34.1: “It’s well, Blessed One, how I understand
snp3.4:34.2: who should eat the offering of one like me,
snp3.4:34.3: whom I should seek at the time of sacrifice
snp3.4:34.4:
having received your advice.”
The Buddha:
snp3.4:35.2: whose mind is limpid, whose sloth is dispelled
snp3.4:36.4: When one like this has come to the sacrifice,
snp3.4:37.1: then, subduing scorn, with hands palm-to-palm
snp3.4:37.4:
In this way the offerings will succeed.”
Sundarika:
snp3.4:38.2: field of merit unexcelled in all the world,
snp3.4:38.3: recipient for all the world deserves the sacrificial cake.
snp3.4:38.4: A gift given to you, master, bears great fruit.
snp3.4:39.1: Then the brahman Sundarika Bhāradvāja said to the Blessed One,
snp3.4:39.3:
Just as if he were to place upright what was overturned, to reveal what was hidden, to show the way to one who was lost,
or to carry a lamp into the dark so that those with eyes could see forms,
in the same way has Master Gotama—through many lines of reasoning—made the Dhamma clear.
snp3.4:39.4: I go to Master Gotama for refuge, to the Dhamma, & to the Saṅgha of monks.
snp3.4:39.5: Let me obtain the Going-forth in Master Gotama’s presence, let me obtain Acceptance (into the Bhikkhu Saṅgha).”
snp3.4:39.6:
Then the brahman Sundarika Bhāradvāja obtained the Going-forth in the Blessed One’s presence, he obtained Acceptance.
And not long after his Acceptance—dwelling alone, secluded, heedful, ardent,
& resolute—he in no long time reached & remained in the supreme goal of the holy life,
for which clansmen rightly go forth from home into homelessness, knowing & realizing it for himself in the here-&-now.
He knew:
“Birth is ended, the holy life fulfilled, the task done.
There is nothing further for the sake of this world.”
snp3.4:39.7: And so Ven. Sundarika Bhāradvāja became another one of the arahants.
